open_positions.title: Will Joey Chestnut win the Men's Division in the 2026 Nathan's Famous Fourth of July International Hot Dog Eating Contest?
Will Miki Sudo win the Women's Division in the 2026 Nathan's Famous Fourth of July International Hot Dog Eating Contest?

bets.title: Will Miki Sudo win the Women's Division in the 2026 Nathan's Famous Fourth of July International Hot Dog Eating Contest?
bets.reasoning: Miki Sudo has been historically dominant in the women's Nathan's contest, with only rare interruption when she did not compete, and the listed challengers have generally not shown the same ceiling. Participation/health and judging variance keep this below certainty, but I make her a materially stronger favorite than 85%. Small, capped position because the orderbook is illiquid/empty.

bets.title: Will Joey Chestnut win the Men's Division in the 2026 Nathan's Famous Fourth of July International Hot Dog Eating Contest?
bets.reasoning: Chestnut is still the clear top men's competitive eater when he participates, and this field's recent Nathan's winners/contenders have generally been well below his established ceiling. The main uncertainty is health/participation and contest-specific variance, but if he is on stage I make him a strong favorite. At an 80c limit my estimate leaves a meaningful edge.

bets.title: Atlanta vs Washington winner?
bets.reasoning: The Atlanta Dream have a more talented young core compared to the rebuilding Washington Mystics. I give Atlanta a 60% probability of winning this matchup, justifying a 55c bid.

bets.title: Argentina vs Cape Verde Winner?
bets.reasoning: Argentina is the defending World Cup champion and a heavy favorite over Cape Verde in this Round of 32 matchup. I estimate an 80% chance for Argentina to win in regulation, making a limit order at 75c a strong value play.

bets.title: Spain vs Austria Winner?
bets.reasoning: Spain boasts a dominant, possession-heavy midfield that should control the pace against Austria. Giving them a 65% probability of winning in regulation provides a solid edge over a 60c limit price.

bets.title: Seattle vs Phoenix winner?
bets.reasoning: The Seattle Storm have a superior roster and home-court advantage against the Phoenix Mercury. I estimate their win probability at 65%, offering value at a 60c limit.

bets.title: Dallas vs Connecticut winner?
bets.reasoning: The Connecticut Sun are consistently one of the strongest defensive teams in the WNBA. They should be clear favorites against the Dallas Wings, meriting a 65% win probability estimate.

notes.text: My limit for new positions this week has been reached (0 remaining). Additionally, I evaluated the orderbooks for my existing 5 open positions (Argentina vs Cape Verde, Spain vs Austria, Seattle vs Phoenix, Dallas vs Connecticut, and Atlanta vs Washington), and found no remaining liquidity to exploit. Therefore, I am passing on making any additional trades this session to wait for the outcomes of my currently held bets.

No new bets this week. I surveyed MLB, WNBA, Wimbledon/tennis, F1/NASCAR, and Nathan's markets, but the actionable candidates showed no quoted prices or orderbook liquidity, so I do not have a reliable market price to compare against my probabilities or a realistic fill path. I am keeping the existing Joey Chestnut and Miki Sudo Nathan's positions and preserving cash rather than forcing illiquid limit orders without a clear edge.

Week of 2026-07-02: PASS. Surveyed the in-season board broadly — today's WNBA games (DAL/CONN, SEA/PHX, ATL/WSH), MLB game moneylines (Jul 4-5 slate), and season-long futures (World Series, NL East division). Every market I inspected returned null bid/ask and an empty orderbook, so there was no market-implied probability to benchmark against and no liquidity to fill against. My discipline requires a measurable edge versus a real price plus a realistic fill; neither exists this session. Rather than lob speculative limit orders into empty books, I'm sitting the week out and preserving the full $100 bankroll for a week with genuine, tradeable edges.

This week, my bankroll indicates that I have already reached my limit of 5 new positions for the week (new_positions_remaining_this_week: 0). I investigated my currently open positions (World Cup games involving Argentina and Spain, and WNBA games involving Seattle, Connecticut, and Atlanta), but the orderbooks for all five markets are completely empty, leaving no liquidity to add to or adjust my stakes. With no actionable edges and hard limits restricting new entries, the optimal and only disciplined move is to pass and sit the week out.

Placed two small paper positions in Nathan's winners: Joey Chestnut YES at 80c (88% forecast) and Miki Sudo YES at 85c (93% forecast). I surveyed MLB/WNBA/CFL/tennis/golf/F1/NASCAR, but most markets showed no displayed bid/ask or empty orderbooks, so I avoided broader action and kept sizing below the 10% per-market cap despite perceived edge.

Week of 2026-07-02: PASSING. Surveyed the board broadly — MLB game winners, 2026 World Cup knockout matches (Spain-Austria, Argentina-Cape Verde, Portugal-Croatia, etc.), WNBA games, and tennis. Every market I inspected returned null bid/ask, no last price, zero volume, and an empty orderbook. With no live prices there is no way to measure an edge against the market, and no resting liquidity to fill against — any order would be a blind bet violating my liquidity and edge discipline. No edge, no liquidity, so I deploy zero capital this week and preserve bankroll for a week with tradeable prices. Passing is a decision, not a failure.

Since the orderbook lacked visible resting liquidity this week, I utilized resting limit orders at my estimated probability value thresholds (leaving room for edge) across 5 independent positions. My strategy focused on capitalizing on strong favorites with structural advantages in two distinct sports: the 2026 World Cup and the WNBA. 

In the World Cup knockout stage, I backed Argentina (vs Cape Verde) and Spain (vs Austria) at 75c and 60c limits respectively, based on their overwhelming talent disparity and pedigree. In the WNBA, I placed bids on the Seattle Storm, Connecticut Sun, and Atlanta Dream—backing the stronger rosters and better form at conservative value thresholds. All trades were sized using 1/4 Kelly criterion to diversify risk and avoid overexposure in low-visibility markets.

Passed this week. I surveyed current MLB, WNBA, Wimbledon/tennis, Nathan's hot dog contest, F1, NASCAR, and PGA markets, but the candidate markets I checked showed no posted bid/ask and empty order books, so there was no reliable price to compare against my probabilities or realistic path to a fill. With no demonstrated 5+ point edge and poor liquidity, preserving bankroll is the disciplined choice.

Week of 2026-07-02: PASSING. Surveyed the in-season markets — MLB game moneylines (Jul 4-5), WNBA games, and Wimbledon ATP/WTA round-of-32 matches. Every market I checked (get_market + get_orderbook across ~8 tickers, including marquee events like Sinner vs Brooksby and Fritz vs Sonego) returned null prices, zero volume, and empty orderbooks. With no resting liquidity and no market-implied price, I have nothing to measure an edge against, and posting deep bids into empty books without a live news feed only invites adverse selection. No edge, no liquidity, so no bets. Cash preserved (100% of $100 bankroll) for a week with tradable prices.
